theme_strategy.core_philosophy: Music is not just heard, it is seen. The UI breathes with the rhythm.

headings.usage: Use for all section titles, artist names, and hero text.

body.usage: Use for track lists, descriptions, and UI controls.

mono.usage: Use for time stamps (0:00 / 3:45) and track numbers.

buttons.primary: bg-primary hover:bg-primary/90 text-white rounded-full px-8 py-3 font-medium transition-all shadow-[0_0_20px_rgba(124,58,237,0.3)] hover:shadow-[0_0_30px_rgba(124,58,237,0.5)]
buttons.secondary: bg-white/10 hover:bg-white/20 text-white rounded-full px-6 py-2 backdrop-blur-md transition-colors
buttons.icon: p-3 rounded-full hover:bg-white/10 text-white/80 hover:text-white transition-colors

cards.album: group relative overflow-hidden rounded-2xl bg-surface/50 hover:bg-surface/80 transition-all border border-white/5 hover:border-white/20 aspect-square
cards.artist: rounded-full aspect-square object-cover border-2 border-transparent hover:border-primary transition-all
cards.track_row: flex items-center gap-4 p-3 rounded-xl hover:bg-white/5 transition-colors group cursor-pointer

player.container: fixed bottom-4 left-4 right-4 h-24 rounded-2xl backdrop-blur-2xl bg-black/60 border border-white/10 shadow-2xl flex items-center justify-between px-6 z-50
player.progress_bar: h-1 bg-white/20 rounded-full cursor-pointer overflow-hidden group hover:h-2 transition-all
player.progress_fill: h-full bg-gradient-to-r from-cyan-400 to-purple-500 rounded-full shadow-[0_0_10px_rgba(6,182,212,0.5)]

lighting_effects.description: The background should subtly pulse with the music's dominant color. Use a large, blurred div behind the main content.
lighting_effects.implementation: Extract dominant color from album art (or use genre default) and animate opacity/scale based on audio amplitude.

textures.noise: Overlay a fixed 'noise' texture at 3% opacity over the entire app to reduce banding and add analog feel.
textures.scanlines: Optional subtle scanline overlay for the visualizer area.

icons.instructions: Use lucide-react for all UI icons (Play, Pause, Skip, Search, Home, Library). Use thin stroke width (1.5px) for elegance.

audio_visualizer.fallback: CSS animation bars if Web Audio fails

instructions_to_main_agent: 2. THEME: Force DARK MODE. Do not implement a light mode toggle. The app is nocturnal.
3. API: Create a service file `api.js` to handle all fetch requests to `https://saavn.sumit.co`. Handle CORS if necessary (or proxy).
4. PLAYER: The player component must be persistent (Global Layout). Use a Context or Zustand store to manage `currentTrack`, `isPlaying`, `queue`.
5. VISUALIZER: Create a `Visualizer` component that connects to the `<audio>` element. Use `useRef` to access the audio context.
6. SEARCH: Implement a debounced search bar in the header. Show results in a dropdown or redirect to /search page.
7. PERFORMANCE: Lazy load all images. Use `react-lazy-load-image-component` or native `loading='lazy'`.
8. ERROR HANDLING: If API fails or returns no image, use the `fallback_album` image provided in assets.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur)
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: 'transition: all'. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add '.App { text-align: center; }' in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮 for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable
Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...)
